Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

the patch looks a bit confusing, imo. it mixes few different problems. there is an issue with limiting a number of RPCs in flight to prevent DoS and this doesn't depend on the type/nature of RPC. there is another limit related to recovery and slot in last_rcvd. I'm not sure the same mechanism can be used.

(3 comments)

Patch Set 6:

Looks good to me.
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 6:

we want to limit total RPCs in flight (with no regard to type), then sometimes we want to limit different types of RPCs. I still don't see how the patch addresses this.

Patch Set 5:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/22691/

Patch Set 6:

Hm... That depends on the RPC callers. Currently, only MDC/FLD/LFSCK RPC will call obd_{get,put}_request_slot(), which match our current status. If we want to control all RPCs, then we can make more components to call these interfaces. This patch only build the interfaces.

(1 comment)

sorry, I missed that it's not read/write semantics anymore.  didn't you consider to make flow control a part of ptlrpc?

Patch Set 6:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/f69ba95c-b53b-11e3-b2ed-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-lfsck.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

It is not impossible to make all the RPC follow control inside ptlrpc, but I still not think carefully about how it will affect kinds of RPC callers, such as client hp I/O. Anyway, this patch can be the first one, and we can implement the finial goal step by step.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

btw, we shouldn't be having >1 modifying RPC in flight. this includes LFSCK and DNE updates - otherwise some updates can be applied >1 time. I guess we should fix this ASAP.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

For LFSCK, it is not important that some RPC are applied more than once, because in spite of re-create or destroy for LFSCK, they are both FID-based, multiple applying the same RPC will neither cause inconstancy nor LFSCK error, the side effect is that the statistics may be not accurate.

On the other hand, if we only allow single LFSCK repairing RPC in flight, then the performance will be very bad. I have ever tested such mode on OpenSFS cluster, it was at most 20% of current performance.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

while this is OK to repeat destroy, I don't think it is for create (ignoring -EEXIST doesn't sound right to me) and clearly for other possible scenarios (especially in LFSCK3).
this is known issue and I do see two possible solutions here:
1) batch more on OSP side
2) more slots in last_rcvd

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Why repeat create for LFSCK will cause trouble? returning "-EEXIST" for LFSCK creating the lost OST-object is acceptable. LFSCK's purpose is to try to make the system in consistent status. As for how (and by whom) the reparation is done is not important. On another words, if we allow multiple LFSCK instances run on the same target in parallel, we will not care about which LFSCK instance fix the inconsistency as long as the lost MDT-object can be re-created.

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

this isn't very nice to repeat and get -EEXIST because we now have to think how to handle -EEXIST: where it is valid or not, in which case, etc. and notice we want to reuse this infrastructure for other features.

the basics of our recovery model is "execute-once". in some very specific cases (like destroy) we can relax the model, but in general we should not. some updates are idempotent (like destroy), but some aren't definitely - like ref_{add|del}.
Uploaded patch set 7.

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-4927 scrub: rebuild stale OST OI mapping

commit.subject: LU-4687 obdclass: unified flow control interfaces
commit.message:
LU-4687 obdclass: unified flow control interfaces

Unify the flow control interfaces for MDC RPC, FLD RPC and lfsck
remote update (via OSP). We allow to adjust the maximum inflight
RPCs count via /proc interface.

Rename some variables/functions to make them easily understood.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I096a1de17fe226bac2fe3a3c891365c2e2c51ef3
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/9562
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
